steps.title: Use the original intersection
steps.content: The two original lines intersect at $(8,2)$. A solution to the three-equation system would have to be this same point and also satisfy the added equation.

steps.title: Test the added equation
steps.content:
Substitute $x=8$ and $y=2$ into $x+4y=-16$:
$$
8+4(2)=16
$$
The left side is $16$, not $-16$, so the original intersection is not on the added line.

steps.title: Count common solutions
steps.content: The original system has only one intersection, and that point fails the added equation. Therefore, no point satisfies all three equations. The answer is A.
